title: Industrial Protocol Monitoring: Unauthorised Function Codes and Anomalous Operations
exclusions: Excludes test-bench protocol traffic in lab environments (filtered via `environment="prod"`). Excludes intra-bay communication on safety-rated SIS network segments that have no IP path; this UC focuses on IP-encapsulated industrial protocols. Excludes proprietary vendor-encrypted protocols which require vendor-specific decoders covered by separate use cases (e.g. Siemens S7 v4+ encrypted).
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.51.14` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `otcc_clause="OTCC-2-12-2-1"`. Each row preserves (protocol, src_ip, src_zone, dest_ip, dest_zone, function_code, function_description, requested_register_range, src_user, src_host, observed_at, src_authorised_flag, function_authorised_flag, register_range_authorised_flag, anomaly_score). Dashboard `NCA OTCC – Industrial Protocol Monitoring` shows per-protocol baseline-deviation trend and unauthorised-write table.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Continuously parses industrial-protocol metadata and validates source authorisation, function-code authorisation, and register-range authorisation against the policy lookup. Combined with UC-22.51.5 (segmentation), this satisfies the industrial-protocol monitoring control.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves industrial protocols are monitored at the function-code level, that only authorised engineering hosts perform write or programme-mode operations, and that anomalous protocol behaviour is detected and investigated.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.51.14` archived to `audit_evidence` plus the `NCA OTCC – Industrial Protocol Monitoring` dashboard.

controlTest.positiveScenario: From a non-engineering host on the OT-DMZ, issue a Modbus function code 16 (Write Multiple Holding Registers) to a test controller. Within one hour UC-22.51.14 MUST surface the event with `src_authorised_flag=false` and `function_authorised_flag=false`, route a notable to SOC, and archive the row.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An authorised engineering workstation issuing function code 3 (Read Holding Registers) to the same controller during an approved engineering window MUST NOT be flagged.

dataSources: Splunk Stream wire data (`stream:modbus`, `stream:dnp3`, `stream:iec104`, `stream:iec61850_goose`, `stream:ethernetip`, `stream:opcua`, `stream:profinet`), Cisco Cyber Vision protocol-decoded events (`cisco:cybervision:flow`, `cisco:cybervision:event`), Claroty CTD protocol events. Lookups: `ot_authorised_writers.csv` (which hosts may perform write/programme operations), `ot_protocol_function_policy.csv` (protocol→allowed function codes per zone pair), `ot_register_policy.csv` (allowed register ranges per controller).
app: Splunk_TA_stream (Splunkbase 1809), Splunk Add-on for Cisco Cyber Vision (Splunkbase 6071), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).

splunkbaseApps.name: Splunk Add-on for Cisco Cyber Vision

spl:
index=stream OR index=cybervision earliest=-2h@h
  (sourcetype="stream:modbus" OR sourcetype="stream:dnp3" OR sourcetype="stream:iec104" OR sourcetype="stream:iec61850_goose" OR sourcetype="stream:ethernetip" OR sourcetype="stream:opcua" OR sourcetype="stream:profinet" OR sourcetype="cisco:cybervision:flow" OR sourcetype="cisco:cybervision:event")
  (function_code=* OR command=* OR service=*)
| eval protocol=case(
    sourcetype="stream:modbus", "modbus",
    sourcetype="stream:dnp3", "dnp3",
    sourcetype="stream:iec104", "iec104",
    sourcetype="stream:iec61850_goose", "goose",
    sourcetype="stream:ethernetip", "ethernetip",
    sourcetype="stream:opcua", "opcua",
    sourcetype="stream:profinet", "profinet",
    sourcetype="cisco:cybervision:flow" OR sourcetype="cisco:cybervision:event", coalesce(protocol, "cybervision"),
    1=1, "unknown")
| eval function_code=coalesce(function_code, command, service)
| eval function_description=case(
    protocol="modbus" AND function_code IN ("1","2","3","4"), "read",
    protocol="modbus" AND function_code IN ("5","6","15","16"), "write",
    protocol="modbus" AND function_code IN ("23"), "read-write",
    protocol="modbus" AND function_code IN ("43"), "identification",
    protocol="dnp3" AND function_code IN ("0","1","2"), "read",
    protocol="dnp3" AND function_code IN ("3","4","5","6"), "write/control",
    protocol="dnp3" AND function_code IN ("23"), "delay measurement",
    protocol="ethernetip" AND service IN ("4c","0e"), "read",
    protocol="ethernetip" AND service IN ("4d","4e","4f"), "write",
    protocol="opcua" AND service="WriteRequest", "write",
    protocol="opcua" AND service="ReadRequest", "read",
    1=1, coalesce(function_description, "other"))
| eval src_zone=coalesce(src_zone, src_purdue_level)
| eval dest_zone=coalesce(dest_zone, dest_purdue_level)
| eval src_ip=coalesce(src_ip, src)
| eval dest_ip=coalesce(dest_ip, dest)
| lookup ot_authorised_writers.csv src_ip OUTPUT src_authorised_flag src_user src_host
| eval src_authorised_flag = coalesce(src_authorised_flag, "false")
| lookup ot_protocol_function_policy.csv protocol src_zone dest_zone function_code OUTPUT function_authorised_flag
| eval function_authorised_flag = coalesce(function_authorised_flag, "false")
| lookup ot_register_policy.csv dest_ip function_code OUTPUT register_range_min register_range_max
| eval register_range_authorised_flag = case(
    function_description NOT IN ("write","read-write"), "true",
    isnotnull(requested_register) AND requested_register >= register_range_min AND requested_register <= register_range_max, "true",
    1=1, "false")
| eval observed_at=_time
| eval anomaly_score = case(
    function_description IN ("write","read-write") AND src_authorised_flag="false", 9,
    function_description IN ("write","read-write") AND function_authorised_flag="false", 8,
    function_description IN ("write","read-write") AND register_range_authorised_flag="false", 7,
    function_description="identification" AND src_authorised_flag="false", 6,
    src_authorised_flag="false" AND function_description="read", 4,
    1=1, 1)
| where src_authorised_flag="false" OR function_authorised_flag="false" OR (function_description IN ("write","read-write") AND register_range_authorised_flag="false")
| stats earliest(observed_at) AS observed_at max(anomaly_score) AS anomaly_score values(function_code) AS function_codes values(requested_register) AS register_range_observed BY protocol src_ip src_zone dest_ip dest_zone function_description src_authorised_flag function_authorised_flag register_range_authorised_flag
| table observed_at protocol src_ip src_zone dest_ip dest_zone function_codes function_description register_range_observed src_authorised_flag function_authorised_flag register_range_authorised_flag anomaly_score
| sort - anomaly_score observed_at
description: Decodes industrial-protocol metadata across Modbus, DNP3, IEC 60870-5-104, IEC 61850 GOOSE, EtherNet/IP, OPC UA, and Profinet, classifies each transaction's function, and flags unauthorised sources, unauthorised function codes for the (src_zone, dest_zone) pair, or write attempts against register ranges outside policy.
value: OTCC inspectors will probe whether OT is monitored 'at the protocol level' rather than only 'at the network level'. A firewall might allow a Modbus connection between two zones, but inside that connection a `Write Multiple Holding Registers` could rewrite a tank-level alarm setpoint. OTCC requires both layers; this UC fulfils the protocol-layer one.
implementation: (1) Deploy Splunk Stream on TAP/SPAN ports at each OT-DMZ and OT-aggregation switch. (2) Onboard Cisco Cyber Vision sensors per UC-22.51.5. (3) Build `ot_authorised_writers.csv` listing each src_ip→src_user→src_host→authorised_flag. (4) Build `ot_protocol_function_policy.csv` defining for each (protocol, src_zone, dest_zone) which function codes are policy-permitted. Reference patterns: Modbus from L3 to L2 only function 3 (read holding) and function 23 (read-write) per OEM guidance; from L4 engineering workstation, all writes permitted only during engineering window. (5) Build `ot_register_policy.csv` per controller declaring allowed write-register ranges (operator setpoints) and forbidden ranges (firmware addresses, safety setpoints).
visualization: (Panel 1) Single-value 'Unauthorised writes in 24h'. (Panel 2) Heatmap of (src_zone, dest_zone, protocol) with anomaly_score colour. (Panel 3) Sortable table of flagged events. (Panel 4) 13-month trend by protocol and anomaly_score. (Panel 5) Function-code distribution by protocol — baseline learning aid.

references.title: ISA/IEC 62443-3-3 — System security requirements (SR) for IACS — SR 5: Restricted data flow

knownFalsePositives:
1. **HMI polling baseline transients** — HMI vendors sometimes change polling cadence; the new pattern can look anomalous. Suppression: tune the function-policy lookup to the new pattern after vendor confirmation.

2. **Diagnostic reads from engineering workstations during commissioning** — commissioning generates atypical read patterns. Suppression: time-bound `commissioning_windows.csv` opens up the policy temporarily.

3. **Firmware identification reads** — Modbus function code 43 / EtherNet/IP service 0x01 may be triggered by asset-inventory tools. Suppression: identify tool source-IPs and add to `ot_authorised_writers.csv` with `function_scope="identify_only"`.

4. **Vendor protocol-conformance test traffic** — during vendor service visits, conformance tests can include unusual function codes. Suppression: tie to a vendor change ticket and time-bound exception.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `otcc_22_51_14_exceptions` keyed on `(protocol, src_ip, dest_ip)` with `expires_at`, `reason`, `approved_by`.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- Splunk Stream (Splunkbase 1809) installed on independent Heavy Forwarders connected to TAP/SPAN ports across the OT-DMZ, the OT-aggregation switches, and the controller-rack switches. The streams to enable: modbus, dnp3, iec104, iec61850_goose, ethernetip, opcua, profinet.
- Splunk Add-on for Cisco Cyber Vision (Splunkbase 6071) for layered passive monitoring at the controller bay.
- `index=stream` and `index=cybervision` (12-month retention), `index=audit_evidence` (already provisioned).
- Lookups:
  - `ot_authorised_writers.csv` — owned by OT cybersecurity engineering. Columns: `src_ip`, `src_user`, `src_host`, `authorised_flag`, `function_scope` (mvfield: which functions the source may perform).
  - `ot_protocol_function_policy.csv` — owned by OT cybersecurity engineering. Reference rows for Modbus:

    ```csv
    protocol,src_zone,dest_zone,function_code,function_authorised_flag
    modbus,L4,L2,3,true
    modbus,L4,L2,16,true
    modbus,L3,L2,3,true
    modbus,L3,L2,16,false
    modbus,L2,L3,3,false
    modbus,L1,L3,16,false
    modbus,L4,L2,23,true
    ```

  - `ot_register_policy.csv` — owned by OT operations engineering, mirrors the controller's tag-address policy. Columns: `dest_ip`, `function_code`, `register_range_min`, `register_range_max`, `register_class` (`operator_setpoint`, `firmware`, `safety`, `diagnostic`).
  - `commissioning_windows.csv`.
  - KV Store `otcc_22_51_14_exceptions`.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

On a Stream Heavy Forwarder, enable the industrial protocol streams in `streams.conf`:

```ini
[stream:modbus]
disabled = 0
ingest_field_extraction = 1
fields = src_ip, dest_ip, function_code, transaction_id, requested_register, register_count, response_status
port = 502

[stream:dnp3]
disabled = 0
fields = src_ip, dest_ip, function_code, application_object, qualifier
port = 20000

[stream:opcua]
disabled = 0
fields = src_ip, dest_ip, service, node_id, requested_value
port = 4840
```

For protocols on non-standard ports, set the port explicitly.

Validate the parsed protocol fields:

```spl
index=stream sourcetype=stream:modbus earliest=-1d
| fieldsummary
| search field IN (function_code, src_ip, dest_ip, requested_register)
```

Volume guidance: a busy OT estate of 200 controllers generates 50–500 GB/day of stream data (dominated by Modbus and EtherNet/IP). Sizing for this UC must include both Stream and Cyber Vision; allow 1–2 GB/day net into the alerting indexes after summary indexing of the read-heavy traffic.

To control volume, configure Stream `aggregate=true` and emit one summary event per `(src_ip, dest_ip, function_code)` per 30-second window for read operations, while preserving full per-event detail for write operations. The SPL above works against the aggregated form; switch the field semantics if needed.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The search has four stages:

1. **Decode**: derive `protocol` and `function_description` from sourcetype and function_code. Modbus function codes 1–4 are reads; 5–6 and 15–16 are writes; 23 is read-write; 43 is device identification.
2. **Source authorisation**: join `src_ip` to `ot_authorised_writers.csv`. Sources not in the lookup are `false`.
3. **Function authorisation**: join `(protocol, src_zone, dest_zone, function_code)` to `ot_protocol_function_policy.csv`.
4. **Register authorisation**: for writes, join `(dest_ip, function_code, requested_register)` to `ot_register_policy.csv`.

Four flags are produced. The search filters rows where any of (`src_authorised_flag=false`, `function_authorised_flag=false`, `register_range_authorised_flag=false`) is true.

**Schedule**:

```ini
[UC-22.51.14: Industrial Protocol Monitoring: Unauthorised Function Codes and Anomalous Operations]
cron_schedule = 25 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -2h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable, email
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.51.14,reg=NCA_OTCC,clause=OTCC-2-12-2-1
action.notable.param.rule_title = NCA OTCC unauthorised industrial-protocol operation
action.notable.param.security_domain = network
action.notable.param.severity = critical
action.email.to = ot-soc@example.com,ot-cyber-programme@example.com
alert.severity = 5
```

Notable creation when `anomaly_score >= 7` (write operations by unauthorised sources, or against forbidden register ranges). Lower scores route via email + audit_evidence.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Stream coverage by protocol**:

```spl
index=stream earliest=-1h
| stats count by sourcetype
```

Every protocol in scope should be non-zero; missing protocols mean Stream config or TAP placement is incorrect.

**(b) Policy lookup health**:

```spl
| inputlookup ot_protocol_function_policy.csv
| stats count by protocol
```

Each in-scope protocol should have policy rows.

**(c) Authorised-writer health**:

```spl
| inputlookup ot_authorised_writers.csv
| stats count by authorised_flag
```

Review annually; document why each `authorised_flag=true` source has write privilege.

**(d) Positive control**: per `controlTest.positiveScenario`. Confirm notable within 1 hour.

**(e) Negative control**: per `controlTest.negativeScenario`. Confirm no flag.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `NCA OTCC – Industrial Protocol Monitoring`.

- **Row 1, single values**: 'Unauthorised writes 24h', 'Out-of-zone function codes 24h', 'Out-of-range register writes 24h'.
- **Row 2, heatmap**: src_zone × dest_zone shaded by anomaly_score.
- **Row 3, sortable table**: every flagged event with drill-through to the Stream raw event and to the asset record for both source and destination.
- **Row 4, function-code distribution by protocol**: baseline learning aid.
- **Row 5, 13-month trend**: KPI for steering committee.

**Runbook for `anomaly_score>=7`** (owner: OT SOC, escalating to OT cybersecurity director):

1. **Within 15 minutes**: contain by isolating the source host or revoking its protocol authorisation at the OT-DMZ firewall.
2. **Within 1 hour**: identify the user. If a known engineer, contact directly to confirm intent. If unknown, treat as a P1 incident and engage SIRT.
3. **Forensic**: preserve all protocol events from the source for 1 hour before and 4 hours after. Capture register values via Cisco Cyber Vision or the historian.
4. **Operational**: confirm with control-room operators that no process state change resulted from the unauthorised write. If yes, engage process-engineering for safety review.
5. **Lessons**: feed into UC-22.51.16 incident detection, UC-22.51.25 training.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Modbus events appear but `function_code` is null** — the Stream parser version is too old for some Modbus dialects. Upgrade `splunk_app_stream` and `Splunk_TA_stream` to latest.

**`requested_register` always null** — Stream may be configured for header-only aggregation; switch to payload-aggregation for Modbus to capture register addresses.

**OPC UA events absent** — OPC UA often uses encrypted tunnels (Sign+Encrypt mode). Stream cannot decrypt; rely on Cisco Cyber Vision for OPC UA visibility, which has vendor-specific decoders. The two streams complement each other.

**OT-specific failure modes**:

- **Cellular-backhauled controllers**: some remote controllers (oil-field RTUs, pumping stations) connect via cellular VPN. Place Stream on the VPN concentrator's egress port rather than at the controller.
- **High-speed deterministic protocols (Profinet IRT)**: nanosecond-deterministic Profinet IRT is too fast for general-purpose Stream sniffing without dedicated TAP hardware. Use Profinet-aware industrial TAPs (e.g. Garland Technology Industrial taps).
- **Vendor-encrypted dialects**: Siemens S7+ with PG/PC firmware ≥4 encrypts the payload; only Cyber Vision or vendor-specific tools provide visibility. Document the encrypted-dialect coverage gap in `audit_evidence` for inspector transparency.
- **Operator HMI page-switch traffic**: when operators flip between HMI pages, an avalanche of read function codes can briefly hit the controller. Tune the read-aggregation window down to 10 seconds for low-volume controllers; expand to 60 seconds for high-volume.
- **GOOSE multicast on layer-2 only segments**: IEC 61850 GOOSE is layer-2-only and never crosses routers. Place Stream on the layer-2 bus directly or rely on the substation's GOOSE-logger appliance (e.g. SIPROTEC) and ingest its log.
grandmaExplanation: We listen to every plant-control message and check it makes sense: was it sent by an approved sender, was it the right kind of command for that area, and did it touch only the settings it was allowed to touch? If something is off we ring the bell loudly.
equipmentModels: Cisco Cyber Vision 4.x (Modbus / DNP3 / IEC 60870-5-104 protocol parsing)
Splunk Stream Independent Forwarder (industrial protocol wire-data capture)
Splunk Add-on for Cisco Cyber Vision (Splunkbase 6071, asset and flow events)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, Network_Traffic data model for tstats)
